methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $15M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $1.8B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Watershed
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $1.8B

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Bedrock Energy is a Series A climate and energy company founded by Joselyn Lai and Silviu Livescu that has raised $15M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 4 same-stage, same-sector private companies (Watershed, Graphyte, Fourth Power, Steady Energy) plus 1 public comparable, yielding a total comp universe of 9 data points across frontier-tech energy infrastructure.

Two triangulation methods apply here. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the $15M total raised as a proxy for entry valuation and applying a typical 2.0x step-up multiplier from Series A to next-stage financing, implies a valuation of $30M. Peer-median analysis across the 4 private comparables yields a median valuation of $175M, with a range spanning $110M to $1.8B. The wide dispersion reflects significant heterogeneity in the climate tech cohort—Watershed commands a $1.8B valuation while Steady Energy sits at $110M—suggesting stage timing and capital intensity drive substantial valuation spreads at this stage.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $26M (low) to $201M (high), with a median of $102M. The substantial gap between stage-progression ($30M) and peer-median ($175M) reflects uncertainty about Bedrock's relative capital efficiency and market positioning within a diverse frontier-energy landscape. Key uncertainties include technology-readiness level relative to peers, capital deployment rate, and path to meaningful revenue. The $102M median represents the center of gravity, but conviction should remain modest given the peer dispersion.
